headline: Marvell Price Target Vaults to $195 at Wells Fargo on AWS Trainium and Custom Silicon Momentum
summary: Wells Fargo delivered one of the most aggressive price target revisions on a major AI semiconductor name this quarter. The firm raised its price target on Marvell Technology (NASDAQ:MRVL) to $195 from $135 and reiterated an Overweight rating, anchoring the call on AWS Trainium deployment and broadening custom silicon momentum. headline: Brokers Suggest Investing in Broadcom Inc. (AVGO): Read This Before Placing a Bet
summary: The average brokerage recommendation (ABR) for Broadcom Inc. (AVGO) is equivalent to a Buy. The overly optimistic recommendations of Wall Street analysts make the effectiveness of this highly sought-after metric questionable. So, is it worth buying the stock?

headline: Is It Too Late To Consider Broadcom (AVGO) After Its Strong One Year Rally
summary: If you are wondering whether Broadcom's current share price still makes sense after its strong run, the next sections will walk through what the numbers say about value. The stock recently closed at US$411.07, with returns of 18.3% year to date and 78.9% over the past year, while the share price has slipped about 2% over the last week and is up 1.1% over the past month. Alongside these price moves, recent coverage has focused on Broadcom's role as a large semiconductor and infrastructure...

headline: Applied Materials Broadcom Alliance Highlights Growing AI Packaging Ambitions
summary: Applied Materials (NasdaqGS:AMAT) has entered a new advanced packaging technology partnership with Broadcom. Broadcom is joining Applied’s EPIC platform to work on advanced chip packaging for next-generation AI systems. The collaboration expands Applied’s network of industry and academic partners focused on AI hardware R&D. Applied Materials sits at the center of the semiconductor equipment and materials market, providing tools that chipmakers use to build and package devices. As AI...

headline: Broadcom Deepens LSEG Ties While Targeting US$100b In Custom AI Chips
summary: Broadcom renewed a five year technology partnership with the London Stock Exchange Group, with LSEG selecting VMware Cloud Foundation for its private cloud and market infrastructure. Under the agreement, Broadcom will also provide professional services to support secure AI and cloud workloads for LSEG. Separately, Broadcom highlighted continued momentum in its custom AI chip business, with an internal revenue target of $100b by 2027. For investors tracking NasdaqGS:AVGO, this news adds...
